query: official Tokyo population 2026 Tokyo Metropolitan Government population estimate
answer: Tokyo's 2026 population is estimated at 36,953,600, a slight decline from 2025. This estimate is based on official projections by the Tokyo Metropolitan Government. The population has been decreasing due to Japan's aging demographic.

results.title: Tokyo, Japan Metro Area Population (1950-2026) - Macrotrends
results.snippet: ## Tokyo, Japan Metro Area Population (1950-2026). ##### The current metro area population of Tokyo in 2026 is **36,954,000**, a **0.22% decline** from 2025. * The metro area population of Tokyo in 2025 was **37,036,000**, a **0.21% decline** from 2024. * The metro area population of Tokyo in 2024 was **37,115,000**, a **0.21% decline** from 2023. * The metro area population of Tokyo in 2023 was **37,194,000**, a **0.21% decline** from 2022. The full historical dataset is available for download here: [Tokyo, Japan Metro Population | Historical Data | 1950-2026](https://www.macrotrends.net/datasets/global-metrics/cities/21671/tokyo/population). ## Total Population.

results.title: Tokyo Has Been Surpassed As The World's Most Populous City
results.snippet: # Tokyo Has Been Surpassed As The World's Most Populous City. Jakarta, the capital of Indonesia, has become the world's most populous city, surpassing Tokyo and Dhaka, according to a new United Nations report. With an estimated population of nearly 42 million, Jakarta has climbed from 33rd place in the 2018 rankings. This shift highlights the rapid urbanization occurring in Asia, where nine out of the ten most populated cities are located. Following Jakarta, Dhaka holds the second spot with 36 million residents and is projected to become the world's largest city by mid-century. Tokyo, which previously held the top position, has seen its population decrease due to Japan's aging and population crisis. The report, titled "World Urbanization Prospects 2025," notes that nearly half of the world's 8.2 billion people now reside in cities, doubling the number of urban dwellers over the past 75 years. By 2050, it is projected that Dhaka will have 52.1 million residents, while Tokyo's population will decline by 2.7 million.

results.title: Tokyo continues to expand as net inflow surpasses 65,000 residents
results.snippet: Photo/Illutration *The Ministry of Internal Affairs and Communications in Tokyo's Chiyoda Ward (Asahi Shimbun file photo)*. Tokyo saw a net increase of 65,219 residents, with the number of people moving to the city exceeding those leaving in the ministry's migration report based on data from the Basic Resident Registration system. Seven prefectures, including Tokyo, recorded population increases, while 40 prefectures saw net outflows. The Tokyo metropolitan area had a cumulative net inflow of 123,534 people; the zone includes the capital as well as Chiba, Kanagawa and Saitama prefectures. The Osaka metropolitan area, which includes Osaka, Hyogo, Kyoto and Nara prefectures, recorded a net inflow of 8,742 people.
